faqs.question: What do plants need to grow healthy and strong?
faqs.answer: Plants need five main things to grow: (1) Sunlight - to make their food, (2) Water - to carry nutrients and stay fresh, (3) Air - to breathe in carbon dioxide, (4) Soil - to get minerals and support, and (5) Space - so their roots and leaves can spread. If even one of these is missing, the plant will not grow well.

faqs.question: How does a seed turn into a plant?
faqs.answer: A seed has a tiny baby plant and some food stored inside it. When the seed gets water and warmth, it breaks open. First, a small root grows downward into the soil. Then a shoot grows upward towards the sunlight. This process is called germination. Slowly the shoot grows leaves and becomes a sapling, and then a full plant with flowers and fruits!

faqs.question: What is compost and how is it made?
faqs.answer: Compost is a natural fertilizer made from kitchen waste and dry leaves. You collect fruit peels, vegetable scraps, tea leaves, and dry leaves in a pot or pit. Over a few weeks, tiny organisms break down this waste into dark, rich soil. This compost is like super food for plants! It helps them grow bigger and healthier without using chemical fertilizers.

faqs.question: When is the best time to water plants and why?
faqs.answer: The best time to water plants is early morning or late evening. If you water in the hot afternoon, the water evaporates (dries up) quickly before the plant can drink it. Morning watering is especially good because the plant gets water for the whole day. Also, do not give too much water because roots can rot if they sit in too much water!

faqs.question: Why are earthworms important for a garden?
faqs.answer: Earthworms are called the farmer's friend because they do many helpful things. As they dig through soil, they create tiny tunnels that let air and water reach plant roots. Their waste (called castings) adds rich nutrients to the soil. They also help mix and loosen the soil, making it easier for roots to grow. A garden with earthworms has much healthier soil!

faqs.question: How do bees and butterflies help plants?
faqs.answer: Bees and butterflies visit flowers to drink a sweet liquid called nectar. While doing this, a yellow powder called pollen sticks to their body. When they fly to another flower, this pollen gets transferred. This is called pollination. Without pollination, many plants cannot make fruits and seeds. That is why bees and butterflies are very important for our gardens and farms!

faqs.question: What vegetables can I grow easily at home?
faqs.answer: Many vegetables grow easily at home, even in small pots! You can try tomatoes, chillies, lady's finger (bhindi), spinach (palak), coriander (dhaniya), and mint (pudina). Herbs like tulsi, curry leaves, and lemongrass also grow well. These plants do not need a lot of space and grow nicely in Indian weather. Start with one or two pots and you will learn quickly!

faqs.question: What are weeds and why should we remove them?
faqs.answer: Weeds are unwanted plants that grow on their own in the garden. They are a problem because they compete with your plants for sunlight, water, and nutrients from the soil. If you do not remove weeds, they can take away all the food and your plants will not grow well. The best way to remove weeds is to pull them out by hand, including their roots, so they do not grow back.

faqs.question: Can plants grow without soil?
faqs.answer: Yes, some plants can grow without soil! The money plant can grow in a glass of water. Some plants like orchids grow on other trees. There is even a method called hydroponics where plants grow in water mixed with special nutrients instead of soil. But most common plants grow best in soil because it gives them support, water, and minerals.

faqs.question: Why did Abdul enjoy gardening so much?
faqs.answer: Abdul enjoyed gardening because he could see seeds turn into plants with his own hands. He learned to be patient and care for living things. He felt happy and proud when his plants grew flowers and vegetables that his family could eat. Gardening also helped him learn about nature, spend time outdoors, and understand how important plants are for all living beings.
